title: Quill3b elevated 5xxs during morning window — customer update
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elaine Chou <elaine.chou@brightmind.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-22T08:14:00-07:00
Subject: Quill3b elevated 5xxs during morning window — quick update

Elaine —

We observed an elevated rate of 5xx and timeout errors for the Quill3b model serving your production traffic between 07:50–08:30 PDT this morning. Error rate peaked at ~12% for token-generation requests and correlated with increased p99 latency. We've attached a Grafana snapshot and a short request trace for your team to review.

Immediate actions we've taken:
- Traffic was temporarily routed to Quill-mini fallback model at 08:12 PDT to reduce customer impact.
- Ops team triggered a rolling restart of the Quill3b runtime pool and disabled a recent kernel patch suspected of increasing KV cache churn.

Requested from BrightMind: can you share whether you saw partial failures on streaming endpoints vs. single-shot calls? A short confirmation will help us narrow the routing logic that hit the error path.

We recognize the impact and are prioritizing full recovery and a short post-incident summary. Naomi is on the escalation and will follow up with technical details.

Thanks for your patience —
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0142

Attachments: grafana-quill3b-503-spike.png (image), quill3b-request-trace-20280522.txt (text)


From: Elaine Chou <elaine.chou@brightmind.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: BrightMind SRE <sre@brightmind.com>
Date: 2028-05-22T08:23:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quill3b elevated 5xxs during morning window — quick update

Ben —

Thanks for the rapid flag. We saw errors across both streaming and non-streaming endpoints; most frequently during longer conversations when the user context was >6k tokens. Our SRE pulled traces and attached a snippet — we’ll forward the trace once processed. Impact: ~8% of production chats returned errors, a small percentage retried successfully.

Can you confirm: when you routed to Quill-mini, were cached prefixes preserved? We saw some duplicates on retries indicating at-least-once semantics; want to minimize user-visible duplicates. Also please let us know ETA for full Quill3b restoration and whether there will be any config changes on our side to avoid future issues.

Elaine Chou
Director, Product Engineering — BrightMind
elaine.chou@brightmind.com


From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-22T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Quill3b elevated 5xxs during morning window — technical notes

Ben / Jonas —

Quick technical summary from Ops (rolling up notes from on-call):
- Timeline: kernel routing optimization (deployed 2028-05-21 22:40 PDT) introduced higher KV cache invalidation under certain seq-len distributions. Under heavier long-context requests (like BrightMind’s >6k use), cache thrash caused higher lock contention and occasional GC of ephemeral caches, which surfaced as 5xx/timeouts.
- Mitigation performed: disabled the kernel change, restarted the Quill3b runtime nodes in a staggered fashion (08:05–08:20 PDT), and temporarily re-routed BrightMind traffic to Quill-mini until nodes passed health checks.
- Observed result: error rate dropped below 0.7% by 08:28 PDT after the last node returned healthy. No data loss is expected; however, we logged a small number of duplicate completions from client-side retries.

Next steps (ops + engineering):
1) Postmortem draft with timeline and root cause (ETA: 48 hours).
2) Backport a conservative cache retention tuning to Quill3b and run a canary set against BrightMind traffic (ETA: 72 hours).
3) Formal rollback policy update to the kernel change with extra preflight tests for long-context distributions.

Action item for Ben: confirm with BrightMind if they want suppressed duplicates via a de-duplication header or prefer us to handle at the infra layer.

Naomi Feldman
Sr. Operations Manager, Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwood.ai

Attachment: quill3b-fallback-plan.pdf (plan summary)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Elaine Chou <elaine.chou@brightmind.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, BrightMind SRE <sre@brightmind.com>
Date: 2028-05-24T17:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quill3b elevated 5xxs during morning window — customer update and next steps

Elaine —

Appreciate the quick confirmation and the trace you shared. Per Naomi’s update, Quill3b is back to normal service and we’ve seen stable error rates in production across the next 36 hours. A few quick notes and asks:

- Duplicate responses: we can add a de-duplication request header (x-redwood-idempotency) for your clients; this is the quickest way to eliminate user-visible duplicates without client changes. Please let me know if we should enable it by default for your account.
- Postmortem: Naomi’s team will publish a 48-hour postmortem to the Drive link below. We will include timeline, root cause, and mitigation/rollback safeguards.
- Follow-up: I propose a 30-minute post-incident call to walk BrightMind SRE through the RCA and the planned canary. Are you available Friday 09:00 PDT?

We sincerely apologize for the disruption and are committed to preventing recurrence. If you prefer a different cadence or want us to open a dedicated Slack channel for realtime ops during the canary, we can set that up.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Solutions, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0142

Links: https://drive.redwood.com/open?id=1QwQuill3b-postmortem

title: Action Required: Complete Third-Party Processing Map for HelixPay Pilot
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@helixpay.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@helixpay.com
Date: Mon, 20 May 2028 09:12:00 +0000
Subject: Initial ask: subprocessor roster + residency details for pilot

Hi Priya,

We're gearing up for the HelixPay pilot next month and procurement/security need the complete list of third-party subprocessors that Redwood will engage for the pilot. Specifically we need:
- Full roster (vendor name + country of processing + function)
- Whether any subprocessors will process cardholder or tokenized payment payloads outside the EU
- Confirmation of contractual mechanism for transfers (SCCs / adequacy / other)
- Any planned temporary access for non-Redwood staff or vendor support

Timeline: we need this packaged by EOD Friday 24 May to meet our review and legal signoff. Also include any evidence (SOC2 reports, KMS/HSM attestations) or a short plan to provide those artifacts.

Can you confirm? If helpful we can share our security questionnaire ahead of the call.

Thanks — Liam
Liam O'Connor
Head of Procurement, HelixPay
liam.oconnor@helixpay.com

---
Attachment: helixpay_security_qs_v1.xlsx (referenced)

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@helixpay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 20 May 2028 13:45:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Initial ask: subprocessor roster + residency details for pilot

Hi Liam —

Thanks for the note and the timeline. We're on it. High-level answers up front:
- We'll attach our current subprocessor inventory (redwood_subprocessors_20280520.csv) with processing country and function flags.
- For the HelixPay pilot, no cardholder PANs will leave EU data centers by default; tokenized payloads may be processed by analytics partners in the US only if you opt into cross-border processing.
- Transfers: we rely on SCCs for cross-border subcontractor relationships that do not have an EU adequacy determination.
- Temporary vendor support: we have a documented temporary-access policy; any third-party break-fix access is logged, time-limited, and requires prior approval from our security team.

I've attached: redwood_subprocessors_20280520.csv (roster + country + role), a DPA draft for your review (dpa_draft_helixpay_v1.docx). Ben and Rishi are cc'd for technical attestations. Marissa will take point on contract language.

Question for you: does HelixPay need SOC2 Type II evidence up-front, or would a SOC2 summary + targeted attestations (KMS/HSM scope + encryption in transit at rest) be sufficient to unblock procurement?

We'll plan to consolidate everything and send a single package by Friday EOD. If you can share your security questionnaire in the meantime that helps prioritize artifacts.

Best,
Priya Natarajan
Senior Manager, Legal & Data Protection
Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: redwood_subprocessors_20280520.csv (CSV, 29 rows); dpa_draft_helixpay_v1.docx (Word)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@helixpay.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 21 May 2028 10:03:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Initial ask: subprocessor roster + residency details for pilot

Liam / team —

Following up on Priya's note with a few technical clarifications from infra/security:
1) KMS/HSM scope: we use customer-isolated keys for Dedicated customers; for hosted pilot customers we offer envelope encryption where Redwood manages root keys in HSM. I'll attach the KMS scoping doc (kms_scope.pdf).
2) SOC2: we can provide the SOC2 Type II report under NDA; alternatively we can produce a redactable evidence bundle (key control, pen test summary, monitoring screenshots) to speed procurement reviews. Which do you prefer?
3) Temporary vendor support: any temporary access for debugging is executed via jumpbox with session recording. If HelixPay requires vendor access to logs or metrics, we'll put in place short-lived tokenized access and an access justification log entry.

If you have specific technical controls you want validated in the next 48 hours, enumerate them and I'll prioritize.

Ben Carter
Head of Security Operations
Redwood Inference

---
Attachment: kms_scope.pdf (PDF, 4 pages)

> Hi Liam —
> 
> Thanks for the note and the timeline. We're on it. High-level answers up front:
> - We'll attach our current subprocessor inventory (redwood_subprocessors_20280520.csv) with processing country and function flags.
> - For the HelixPay pilot, no cardholder PANs will leave EU data centers by default; tokenized payloads may be processed by analytics partners in the US only if you opt into cross-border processing.
> - Transfers: we rely on SCCs for cross-border subcontractor relationships that do not have an EU adequacy determination.
> - Temporary vendor support: we have a documented temporary-access policy; any third-party break-fix access is logged, time-limited, and requires prior approval from our security team.
> 
> I've attached: redwood_subprocessors_20280520.csv (roster + country + role), a DPA draft for your review (dpa_draft_helixpay_v1.docx).


From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@helixpay.com>
To: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@helixpay.com, security@helixpay.com
Date: Wed, 22 May 2028 08:30:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Initial ask: subprocessor roster + residency details for pilot

Priya / Ben —

Appreciate the quick turnaround. A few follow-ups from HelixPay security/procurement:
- SOC2 under NDA is preferred; procurement will accept a redacted version if we can validate key controls. Please send the NDA link or we can share ours.
- KMS: we need clarity on whether customer-derived keys or Redwood-managed keys are used for pilot tokens. If Redwood manages root keys, confirm export controls and whether keys ever transit outside EU HSM zones.
- Subprocessor details: one of the entries in your CSV lists an analytics vendor flagged as US processing; can you confirm whether that vendor receives tokenized data (no PAN) or any de-identified transaction context that could be used to re-identify?

We can take the DPA draft and mark redlines. I can share our security questionnaire but prefer a short call to confirm technical constraints. Are you available next week for a 30-minute technical sync? Our availability: Tue 28 May 09:00-11:00 CET or Wed 29 May 14:00-16:00 CET.

Also attaching our redlines file: helixpay_dpa_redlines_20280522.docx (referenced).

Cheers,
Liam

---
Quoted message follows.


From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@helixpay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 24 May 2028 16:40:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Initial ask: subprocessor roster + residency details for pilot

Thanks Liam —

We've consolidated the materials and attached the package you requested. Highlights:
- Subprocessor roster (CSV) updated with an explicit column for whether tokenized transaction context is shared; the analytics partner marked US receives only aggregated telemetry and hashed token IDs, not PAN or re-identifying raw data.
- KMS: for hosted pilots we manage root keys in EU HSM clusters unless a customer requests a dedicated key. Root keys do not leave EU HSMs; envelope keys for partner integrations are scoped and rotated regularly. See kms_scope.pdf for the KMS boundary.
- Evidence: we can provide a SOC2 Type II report under NDA (link below) and a targeted evidence bundle for immediate review.
- DPA: we've accepted most of your redlines; Marissa has flagged two sections (data export approvals; subprocessor notice period) for quick alignment.

Attachments included: redwood_subprocessors_20280524.csv (updated), dpa_draft_helixpay_v1_marked.docx, kms_scope.pdf, SOC2-summary-redwood_202704.pdf (redactable).

Proposed call times to finalize items: Tue 28 May 09:30-10:00 CET or Wed 29 May 15:00-15:30 CET. Please confirm which slot works and we'll send a calendar invite.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b-helixpay-pilot-pkg
NDA for SOC2 access: https://redwoodinference.sharepoint.com/nda/soc2-request

Best regards,
Priya Natarajan
Senior Manager, Legal & Data Protection
Redwood Inference

---
Attachments: redwood_subprocessors_20280524.csv, dpa_draft_helixpay_v1_marked.docx, kms_scope.pdf, SOC2-summary-redwood_202704.pdf

title: Order form — bandwidth commit safety-net rewrite
preview:
From: Acme Cloud Legal <contracting@acmecloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Mon, 20 May 2028 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Redline: Order Form — commit safety-net language

Ben / Marissa,

Thanks for taking the call last week. Attached is Acme Cloud's mark of the Order Form (ACME_OrderForm_Redline_20280520.pdf) — the primary change is new language we added to the pricing exhibit to protect against volatility in our bandwidth usage. High level: we want a "safety-net" that guarantees a minimum effective rate if our committed bandwidth drops below the pledged tier during the first 12 months, plus a carry-forward credit for occasional usage spikes.

Key asks in the redline:
- Replace the proposed "dynamic true-up" with a three-tier smoothing (30 / 60 / 90 day windows) that caps credits at 25% of monthly committed units.
- Add a phase-in protection for year one so price floor won't reprice retroactively for the first two quarters.
- Explicit carveout for force majeure usage spikes (manual approval required).

We noted Redwood's earlier comments on maintaining predictability for committed capacity. Our proposed language tries to balance that risk with an operational buffer so we aren't hit with steep true-up bills during the seasonal ramp. Please review and flag anything that would be an uptime or capacity risk on your side.

Attachments: ACME_OrderForm_Redline_20280520.pdf (redline), pricing-exhibit-diff.txt (plain diff)

Sanjay Mehta
Head of Contracts, Acme Cloud
contracting@acmecloud.com

---

On Mon, May 20, 2028 at 11:15 AM Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com> wrote: (included for context)
> Thanks — we'll review and get back with a consolidated set of edits.


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Mehta <sanjay@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 May 2028 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Redline: Order Form — commit safety-net language

Sanjay —

Appreciate the redline and the context. Quick read-through from our side; high-level reactions below and we'll attach a counter-mark shortly:

1) The 30/60/90 smoothing tiers are reasonable as a concept, but capping credits at 25% of committed units may still create unit-cost variance for our GPU scheduling. Would you consider a smaller cap (15%) with the remainder handled via a one-time carry-forward credit?

2) The year-one phase-in looks negotiable, but blanket non-retroactivity creates exposure if usage collapses. Can we agree to a "soft floor" for Q1 that converts to the final floor after the 60-day true-up?

3) Force majeure carveouts are fine but need a clear definition and a manual approval SLA (72 hours) to avoid capacity surprises.

I'll ask Marissa (legal) to draft precise language, Karthik to check the operational impact on burst capacity, and Lauren to confirm the proposed discount/credit mechanics from a finance perspective. Our counter-mark will try to preserve your smoothing intent while protecting Redwood's committed-capacity economics. Expect the draft by tomorrow EOD.

Ben
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

Attachment: BEN_Counter_Redline_20280520.docx (will follow)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Mehta <sanjay@acmecloud.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Redline: Order Form — commit safety-net language (track changes)

Sanjay / team —

Attached are inline edits; below are the sections where I recommend different wording rather than wholesale acceptance of your proposals. I put suggested legal copy in brackets for ease of review.

1) "Smoothing and Credits" (Pricing Exhibit) — Proposed edit: "During the 90-day reconciliation window, credits derived from underutilization shall be limited to a cumulative amount not to exceed fifteen percent (15%) of monthly committed units, provided that any unused credits beyond this cap may be carried forward as a one-time balance to the immediately following billing period, subject to a single 60-day true-up."

2) "Phase-in Protection" — Proposed edit: "Notwithstanding the foregoing, for the first two (2) billing periods following contract execution, the Parties agree that adjustments to the effective rate shall be prospective only and shall not be applied retroactively to prior invoices, except in cases of manifest error or fraud."

3) "Force Majeure and Bursts" — Proposed edit: "Temporary usage surges attributable to force majeure events may be eligible for adjustment upon mutual written consent and subject to Redwood's ability to meet capacity without degrading service to other customers; Redwood shall respond to such requests within seventy-two (72) hours."

I kept the language targeted to preserve predictability for both sides. If Acme wants a larger carry-forward pot, that should be scoped to a narrow list of events (seasonal marketing campaigns, acquisitions) rather than day-to-day usage dips.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marissa@redwood.com

Attachment: MARISSA_edited_redline_20280521.pdf


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Sanjay Mehta <sanjay@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 22 May 2028 10:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: RE: Redline: Order Form — commit safety-net language

Quick product/ops input here — thanks for the thoughtful language, Marissa. A few operational constraints and suggestions:

- If we allow carry-forward credits to be applied in a subsequent period, we need to tie the dollar value to consumed tokens (or bandwidth units) at the time the credits are earned. Floating-rate carry-forwards are operationally complex.

- Cap on burst credits: we can support temporary bursts up to the customer's reserved pool, but suggest we limit auto-approved burst credits to 20% of committed throughput per month; anything above should require manual sign-off. That meshes with Marissa's 72-hour approval SLA.

- Rolling window: 90-day reconciliation is fine, but for fast-growth customers we should allow an optional quarterly rebaseline so they can move to a higher committed tier without triggering downside true-ups.

Operational ask: confirm whether carry-forward credits should be token-based or dollar-based, and whether they can offset overage invoices or only future committed fees. My preference is token-based (simpler mapping to our scheduler).

Karthik
Director, Product Operations
karthik@redwood.com


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Sanjay Mehta <sanjay@acmecloud.com>
Date: Wed, 22 May 2028 15:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: RE: Redline: Order Form — commit safety-net language (finance perspective)

Thanks all — finance perspective below.

- Discount math: a 15% credit cap with a one-time carry-forward seems reasonable if carry-forwards are token-based and expire within the following billing cycle. That keeps working capital predictable.

- True-up timing: move to a 30-day active true-up + 60-day settlement period — this reduces our AR exposure and aligns invoicing cadence.

- Liability cap: we should keep a cumulative liability cap tied to the initial 12-month committed value (suggest 150% of average monthly committed fees) to prevent open-ended exposure if usage collapses.

If Acme accepts token-based carry-forwards and the liability cap, we can include the language Marissa suggested and circulate a final for signature.

Lauren Bishop
Head of Finance, Redwood Inference
lauren@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Sanjay Mehta <sanjay@acmecloud.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 23 May 2028 16:45:00 -0700
Subject: Final: Order Form language — proposed agreement and next steps

Sanjay —

Thanks for the collaborative redline and for being flexible on the mechanics. Summary of the items we can agree to and will put into the final Order Form:

1) Smoothing/credits: a 90-day reconciliation window. Credits limited to fifteen percent (15%) of monthly committed units; any credits beyond the 15% cap may be carried forward as a one-time token balance to the immediately following billing period and must be used within the subsequent billing cycle.

2) Phase-in protection: prospective-only adjustments for the first two billing periods post-execution, except for manifest error or fraud.

3) Burst & force majeure: auto-approved burst up to 20% of monthly committed throughput; excess requires mutual written consent. Force majeure requests subject to a 72-hour review SLA.

4) Finance mechanics: carry-forwards token-based, apply to future committed fees only, 30-day active true-up with a 60-day settlement window, and a liability cap equal to 150% of average monthly committed fees for the first 12 months.

Attached is our consolidated mark (ACME_OrderForm_FinalDraft_20280523.docx). If this matches Acme's expectations, please loop in your signatory and we'll send a DocuSign envelope (linked below) for countersignature.

DocuSign (pre-populated): https://app.docusign.com/envelope/1234abcd
HubSpot deal: hubspot:deal/67890

Next steps: Acme to review and confirm by EOD Friday; Redwood legal will prepare countersign version upon confirmation.

Best,

Ben Carter
Head of Sales, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

Attachments: ACME_OrderForm_FinalDraft_20280523.docx

title: Post-discovery: lock technical runbook session (60-90m) & agenda
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Gomez <alex.gomez@atriahealth.com>

Date: Wed, 22 May 2028 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Post-discovery: lock technical runbook session (60-90m) & agenda

Thanks again for the call earlier — good to meet you and the Atria team. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can get your ML/infra folks in a room with our ops/serving engineers.

Recap (short):
- Main objective: validate serving pattern for patient-screening pipeline (low-latency, inference at up to 15 QPS) and review data flows + security constraints.
- Must-haves: VPC deployment option, KMS integration, retention/audit log detail for compliance team.
- Nice-to-have: warm-cache strategies and batching thresholds to optimize cost.

Proposal: 60–90 minute technical runbook session with our infra engineers + your ML/infra leads. Agenda (draft):
1) 10m — Goals & current infra overview (Atria)
2) 20m — Redwood runtime & architecture mapping to your flow
3) 25–35m — Deep dive: KV cache, batching, quantization tradeoffs
4) 10–15m — Security/compliance: KMS, logs, isolation model
5) 10m — Action items & mutual next steps

Please confirm:
- Preferred duration (60 / 75 / 90 minutes)
- Up to three proposed time windows next week (include timezone)
- Which Atria attendees should be present (titles/roles) — we'll map Redwood roster accordingly.

I'll attach a short runbook stub and a SOC2 summary we often get asked for; happy to add any preread notes you want the team to see before the session.

Thanks,
Ben

redwood.ai

title: Re: SparkTrail — demo request and traffic details
preview:
From: Alisha Raman <alisha.raman@sparktrail.io>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 20 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SparkTrail — quick demo request

Hi Avery,

We signed up through your self-serve flow and I wanted to get a demo scheduled this week to see how Redwood could power our contextual search + complaint summarization use cases. Quick context:
- Use case: customer support search (vector search for KB) + on-the-fly thread summarization for CS reps.
- Current traffic: ~1.1M search queries/month, avg prompt ~120 tokens, typical response ~250 tokens.
- Peak: ~18 qps currently; expect growth to ~3M queries/month and ~60 qps in 3–4 months.
- Timeline: pilot in the next 2–3 weeks, production rollout targeted in ~3 months.

Two asks: 1) a short demo focused on throughput/latency and deployment options (hosted vs dedicated), and 2) any quick guidance on expected cost per 1M requests for similarity search workflows.

I can include our compliance lead if needed. Available windows: Tue/Wed mornings PST.

Thanks —
Alisha Raman
Product Manager, SparkTrail
[Attachment: sparktrail_traffic_snapshot.xlsx (XLSX, 42 KB)]

From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Alisha Raman <alisha.raman@sparktrail.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 20 May 2028 10:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SparkTrail — quick demo request

Hi Alisha —

Thanks for the details and for signing up. Happy to run a focussed 30-minute session that covers: architecture options (hosted/dedicated/private), perf expectations for vector search, and a quick look at cost levers (batching, caching, quantization).

Before we lock a time, a couple quick clarifying questions that help us tailor the demo:
- Is data residency a hard requirement (do you need EU-only or specific cloud region)?
- Are you planning to use Redwood API calls from a backend service or embed a private deployment (VPC) in prod?
- Any PII/sensitive data in your KB we should treat specifically (we'll flag for compliance).
- Do you have an SSO/SCIM requirement for pilot access?

Proposed 3 option windows (PT):
1) Tue May 21 10:00–10:30 AM PT
2) Wed May 22 11:00–11:30 AM PT
3) Wed May 22 3:00–3:30 PM PT

You can pick one or drop a preferred time here: https://cal.redwood.com/avery/sparktrail-demo

I attached our quick pilot intake template — filling a few rows (traffic, sample prompts, security notes) speeds up the session.

Best,
Avery Johnson
SDR, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: pilot_template.csv (CSV, 6 KB)]

To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: compliance@sparktrail.io
Date: Tue, 21 May 2028 08:47:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: SparkTrail — quick demo request

- Data residency: US-only is fine for now.
- Deployment: pilot via your hosted API; likely Dedicated for prod but want to validate hosted performance first.
- PII: some KB entries contain email addresses and order IDs; nothing that requires special handling beyond standard controls.
- SSO: yes, we'll need SSO for production access. Pilot can use invite links.

Alisha

We're kicking off our audit intake for Applynx's Private deployment evaluation. The auditor has asked for: 1) an annotated network topology that highlights tenant demarcation for Dedicated and Private modes, 2) a short narrative (1 page) describing isolation controls and control-plane egress points, and 3) SIG/CAIQ mappings or pointers to the SOC 2 evidence bundle.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Subject: Applynx audit packet: deployment guardrails, topology vignettes, and evidence links

Laura — attaching the initial packet for your auditor. Quick summary below and a link to the shared Drive where we've grouped the artifacts. If you want us to walk through any diagram or the CAIQ mapping, we're available Wed 5/24 or Thu 5/25 for a 30-minute session.

Attachments included (brief descriptors):
- deployment_guardrails_diagram_v3.png (annotated topology: Dedicated vs Private overlays, egress choke points, control-plane separation)
- tenant_demarcation_table.xlsx (rows: tenant class, boundary controls, allowed interconnects, logging/retention notes)
- SOC2_cover_letter_Applynx.pdf (cover letter that references the SOC 2 controls and where to find the evidence)

Key points called out in the narrative:
- Tenant demarcation: Dedicated runs in isolated GPU pools with logical tenant overlays; Private deploys run in customer VPC with our private control-plane proxy. 
- Control-plane egress: All control-plane telemetry and management traffic egress through regional control-plane endpoints; approved interconnects are listed in the diagram.
- BYOK/KMS: Private deployments support customer-managed KMS (bring-your-own-key) via our KMS integration. The narrative includes the required handshake steps for key rotation and proof-of-possession.

Drive link (read-only for your auditor): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aB2_example_shared_drive_link
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/123456/deal/APP-98765

Next step: Please confirm if the auditor needs any live network captures or config snippets (they often do not for topology). If they want a live demo, we'd propose Wed 5/24 10:00 AM PT (we'll send a calendar invite).

Thanks — looking forward to closing this out quickly.

Best regards,
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive, Enterprise
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
[Attachments: deployment_guardrails_diagram_v3.png, tenant_demarcation_table.xlsx, SOC2_cover_letter_Applynx.pdf]

title: Ready to transfer: Veridian Health deployment relay
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Miller <laura.miller@veridianhealth.com>, Sam Ortiz <sam.ortiz@veridianhealth.com>
Date: 2028-05-18 09:12 -0700
Subject: Ready to transfer: Veridian Health deployment relay

Team —

We closed Veridian (VER-2345) last week and theyre good to start the formal onboarding next Tuesday. This note is the sales -> success relay with the key context so CS can pick up scheduling and technical onboarding. TL;DR: 6-week pilot with production-lite traffic, one primary integration (EMR writeback via SFTP), and a soft SLA for 99.5% inference availability during business hours.

Key deal points and asks:
- Start window: proposed kickoff week of 2028-05-22 (client prefers Tue/Wed).
- Primary contact: Laura Miller (shell handle scheduling + approvals).
- Data residency: US-only; no cross-border processing.
- Expected concurrency: peak 40 concurrent sessions, avg 12 sessions.
- Success metrics: latency p95 < 450ms, embedding accuracy baseline set by their eval set (see acceptance-criteria.csv).

Technical notes (from presales):
- SFTP for batch EMR uploads, nightly batch of delta files.
- They requested KV-prefix caching for repeated patient lookup flows; recommend conservative cache TTL initially (5m).
- Network: VPC peering is not required; we will use our hosted endpoints behind IP allowlist (network_requirements.xlsx attached).

Attachments: SOW_v1.3.pdf (updated pricing), network_requirements.xlsx, acceptance-criteria.csv. DocuSign link sent to Laura yesterday (see related_links). HubSpot deal id: 2345.

Risks/open items for CS to resolve with client:
1) SSO: client prefers SAML but hasnt confirmed IdP metadata.
2) Data retention: they asked for 90-day logs retention; need to confirm internal retention setting.
3) Cant guarantee dedicated capacity initially — theyre on a reserved burst plan; plan for auto-fallback to the lower-tier model if load spikes.

Recommended owners:
- Primary CS lead: Tessa Morgan (technical onboarding + runbook)
- Customer success sponsor: Marissa Cole (relationship lead, post-pilot expansion)
- Escalation point (sales): Ben Carter

Next steps: Marissa/Tessa to propose firm kickoff times and an initial 30/60/90 mutual action plan by EOD Friday 2028-05-19.

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0147

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Miller <laura.miller@veridianhealth.com>, Sam Ortiz <sam.ortiz@veridianhealth.com>
Date: 2028-05-18 10:05 -0700
Subject: Re: Ready to transfer: Veridian Health deployment relay

Ben — thanks for the concise brief, very helpful. Quick clarifying questions so we can scope the pilot sprint properly: 
1) For the SFTP EMR writeback, do they expect schema transformation by Redwood or will Veridian hand us normalized files?
2) Are they ok with initial model quantization (we recommended int8 for cost) or do they require fp16 for the pilot?

Ill draft a 30/60/90 MAP that includes staging tasks (credentials, sample payloads, acceptance evals) and route it back to the thread. Planning to propose Tue 05/23 10am PT for the kickoff — Laura, does that work?

—Marissa
Marissa Cole | Sr. Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Miller <laura.miller@veridianhealth.com>, Sam Ortiz <sam.ortiz@veridianhealth.com>
Date: 2028-05-18 11:20 -0700
Subject: Re: Ready to transfer: Veridian Health deployment relay

Marissa — answers inline below. Also attaching the finalized SOW and network spreadsheet for reference.

> 1) For the SFTP EMR writeback, do they expect schema transformation by Redwood or will Veridian hand us normalized files?
They will send nightly CSVs with a simple mapping. Veridians integration lead confirmed they will share the schema and sample files before kickoff; we (Redwood) are responsible for a trimming/validation step (not full ETL).

> 2) Are they ok with initial model quantization (we recommended int8 for cost) or do they require fp16 for the pilot?
They told me theyre fine with int8 for the pilot; they want a performance review at the end of the pilot to decide whether to keep int8.

Attachments: SOW_v1.3.pdf (price and payment terms), network_requirements.xlsx. DocuSign link: https://app.redwood.com/docuSign/VER-2345. HubSpot: hubspot:deal:2345.

Ben

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Miller <laura.miller@veridianhealth.com>
Date: 2028-05-18 14:50 -0700
Subject: Re: Ready to transfer: Veridian Health deployment relay

Thanks for the context — adding the technical onboarding checklist and initial owner assignments. Moving Tessa into primary engineering-runbook lead for staging. 

Staging checklist (first pass):
- Collect IdP metadata if SSO confirmed (SAML) — owner: Laura (client) / Tessa (Redwood).
- Provide sample SFTP payload format and test account — owner: Veridian (Sam) / Tessa (Redwood).
- Create staging workspace: model variant pinned to int8 (per Ben) — owner: Karthik / infra team.
- Set up KV-prefix cache with TTL=300s for user lookup endpoints — owner: Karthik.
- Configure monitor alerts (latency p95, error rate) and link to Veridian Slack channel — owner: Tessa.
- Confirm retention settings (90-day logs) and document exception if not possible — owner: Marissa.

Owners:
- Tessa Morgan: technical onboarding, runbook, staging signoff.
- Marissa Cole: customer comms, acceptance criteria, go/no-go decision.
- Ben Carter: pricing/renewal conversations post-pilot.

Note on capacity: We dont have Dedicated reserved capacity for this account initially. Karthik will prepare a conservative autoscaling policy and a fallback model variant to keep costs predictable.

Karthik

From: Laura Miller <laura.miller@veridianhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-05-19 08:02 -0700
Subject: Re: Ready to transfer: Veridian Health deployment relay

Thanks — appreciate the clear owners and checklist. Tue 05/23 10am PT works for our team. A couple quick asks before kickoff: 
1) Can you provision a short-lived test SFTP account this week so our engineers can send a sample file?
2) We need an updated contact card for the escalation chain (phone + backup).

Quick confirmation: we are okay with int8 for pilot and will share IdP metadata by EOD Monday. Ill sign the DocuSign SOW today.

Best,
Laura Miller

title: Finalizing payment rails for BlueHarbor — ACH remittance tag + PO mapping
preview:
From: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.inference.com>
To: Dana Ruiz <payments@blueharborlogistics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.inference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.inference.com>
Date: Thu, 16 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Finalizing payment rails for BlueHarbor — ACH remittance tag + PO mapping

Hi Dana —

Thanks again for sending the onboarding packet yesterday. We have two quick asks before we flip the live flag on payments from Redwood: an ACH remit-field example and the PO->remit mapping we should expect on your side.

What we need from you to finish onboarding today:
- Confirm the exact remittance field you will populate for ACH (we prefer a single text field we can map into our AP reference; example: \"REMIT_NOTE: PO123456 / INV7890\")
- Confirm whether your reconciliation team reads the entire ACH \"addenda\" field or just the remittance line on the payment.
- Confirm wire instructions are acceptable for one-off urgent payouts (we will primarily use ACH).

Attached: W9 + wiring instructions you sent — we copied the file to our vendor record. If you can reply with one sample ACH file line or screenshot of the values your bank provides, that will speed this up.

Goal: map \"PO number\" on our PO to a single consumable remittance token on your side so our finance team can auto-apply payments.

Thanks,

Tessa Morgan

Hi Tessa,

Thanks — happy to confirm. BlueHarbor will appear on ACH files like this (ACH CCD + Addenda):
- ACH addenda (X12-like free text) will contain: \"RBW:PO-<PONUMBER>|INV-<INVOICE>#|REF:<REFERENCE>\"
- Our AR system parses the addenda looking for the \"PO-\" token and then \"INV-\" as fallback.

Example addenda text we see:
RBW:PO-43219|INV-7890|REF:RH-202804

We do read the addenda field for CCD entries — please include PO- prefix and we will match it automatically.

Wire instructions are attached in the PDF as you already have. For urgent wires please use the bank reference field to include the same RBW token above.

Attached: BlueHarbor_W9_and_Wire_Instructions.pdf

—Dana Ruiz

title: Request: Data protection controls, DPA annex and DPF evidence for SigmaCare RFP
preview:
From: Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@sigmacare.co>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lara Chen <l.chen@sigmacare.co>
Date: Mon, 14 May 2028 09:12:00 -0400
Subject: SigmaCare RFP — data protection controls + DPF evidence request

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team was impressed with the routing controls and the quantization options. As part of our procurement packet we need a short package to satisfy our privacy and legal teams. Can you provide the following by end of week?

1) DPA annex (processor terms) that reflects cross-border transfers to US and APAC regions;
2) A concise control matrix showing which technical safeguards map to the EU DPF (Article 46 alternatives + SCCs fallback);
3) Evidence artifacts (logs, example audit entries, KMS integration diagram) — redacted is fine;
4) Confirmation on whether Redwood will accept controller-to-controller transfer restrictions if we request a restricted routing policy (e.g., EU-only) in the contract.

We have a signature page for a mutual action plan and would like to align timelines. If you need to loop in legal/security, please do.

Best,
Tom

[Signature]
Tom Reynolds
Procurement Lead, SigmaCare
[Attachment: SigmaCare_RFP_TOC.pdf (PDF, 86 KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@sigmacare.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 14 May 2028 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SigmaCare RFP — data protection controls + DPF evidence request

Hi Tom — thanks, happy to get this over.

Quick summary of what we'll share by Friday:
- Redwood_DPA_Annex_v1.3.pdf (processor annex covering SCCs and our standard DPA clauses) — Laura will review final legal wording;
- crossborder_control_matrix.xlsx (mapping of safeguards: KMS, TLS, data residency options, model routing, KV cache limits, audit trails);
- DPF_evidence_pack.zip (sample logs, redacted KMS integration diagram, summary of fallback routing policies).

On the question about controller-to-controller restrictions: we can implement a contract-level routing constraint (EU-only) as a configurable policy for Dedicated and Private customers; for Hosted customers we offer region pinning and policy enforcement but true geo-fencing for model inference requires a Dedicated/Private plan. I'll let Laura and Aditya provide the precise contractual language and technical caveats.

Attaching the draft DPA annex and control matrix for initial review.

Best,
Ben

[Attachments: Redwood_DPA_Annex_v1.3.pdf, crossborder_control_matrix.xlsx]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@sigmacare.co>
Date: Tue, 15 May 2028 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SigmaCare RFP — data protection controls + DPF evidence request

Tom / Ben — Laura here from legal. I reviewed the attached DPA annex and added two comments inline:

1) Cross-border transfers clause: we include SCCs as fallback and a DPF compliance statement where we confirm the measures (technical/organizational) we have in place. We can accept a routing restriction as a contractual SLA for Dedicated/Private deployments; for Hosted we can add a best-efforts routing covenant but cannot guarantee absolute data residency unless a Dedicated/Private option is procured.

2) KMS key custody: Redwood supports customer-managed keys (CMKs) via supported KMS providers (AWS KMS, GCP KMS). We will provide a KMS integration diagram in the evidence pack and redacted audit logs showing envelope encryption events.

Next steps: Ben — please upload the DPF_evidence_pack.zip to the shared drive link and I will attach a short legal cover note describing the limitations and what our SOC2/Security attestations include. Tom — if SigmaCare requires contractual language that restricts processing to EU controllers only, send the proposed clause and we'll flag operational impacts.

Laura Bennett
Head of Legal, Redwood Inference
[Attachment referenced inline: Redwood_DPA_Annex_v1.3.pdf (annotated comments)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@sigmacare.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Lara Chen <l.chen@sigmacare.co>
Date: Thu, 17 May 2028 14:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SigmaCare RFP — data protection controls + DPF evidence request

Tom / Lara — uploaded the evidence bundle and a short ops note to the shared drive. Key highlights in the DPF_evidence_pack.zip:

- Redacted audit trail (sample) with token/route metadata (time, model_id, region) — shows routing fallback to EU region on capacity constraints;
- KMS integration diagram (redacted) plus example envelope encryption log entries;
- Control matrix mapping (crossborder_control_matrix.xlsx) covering SCCs, contractual measures, technical mitigations (TLS, in-transit KV cache encryption), and our fallback policies;
- SOC2 report pointer (we do not include full report in the packet; we can provide under NDA).

Also attaching the SigmaCare mutual action plan signed by our VP of Sales (placeholder) — if you confirm countersignature we will route legal for signature.

I note that operationalizing strict EU-only controller processing will require Dedicated capacity (we discussed briefly in the demo). We can lock routes and disable cross-region fallbacks on Dedicated/Private installs; this is a billable change and has an implementation lead time (2-4 weeks) depending on the region.

Let me know if the pack covers items you need for procurement.

Best,
Ben

[Attachments: DPF_evidence_pack.zip, SigmaCare_Mutual_Action_Plan_Signed.docx]

From: Tom Reynolds <t.reynolds@sigmacare.co>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Lara Chen <l.chen@sigmacare.co>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 18 May 2028 16:47:00 -0400
Subject: Re: SigmaCare RFP — data protection controls + DPF evidence request

Ben / Laura / Aditya — received, thank you. The evidence pack is comprehensive and the DPA annex looks good overall. Two quick follow-ups from our legal team:

1) They will propose an EU-only processing clause for the mutual action plan (we will share redline by Mon EOD). Can you confirm Redwood will accept that clause for a Dedicated deployment (we understand there may be additional cost)?

2) For the audit logs: can Redwood provide a sample redacted log entry in CSV format showing the fields your security team recommends we request in the contract (timestamp, model_id, region, sample_request_id, action_taken)? That will help our procurement checklist.

Assuming we can get those items early next week, we aim to circulate a signed mutual action plan the following week and move to contract. Appreciate the quick turnaround.

Best regards,
Tom

[Signature]
Tom Reynolds
Procurement Lead, SigmaCare
[Attachment: SigmaCare_DueDiligence_Checklist.xlsx (Excel, 24 KB)]

title: Aligning the demo: availability ladder + 15m buffer windows
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Karen Liu <karen.liu@auroraai.com>, Rajiv Menon <rajiv@pi-analytics.com>, Satoshi Ito <s.ito@zenbridge.jp>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-05-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Aligning the demo: availability ladder + 15m buffer windows

Hi Karen / Rajiv / Satoshi —

Thanks again for coordinating — we want to lock a single executive-friendly demo that includes a short 20-minute exec walkthrough followed by a 40-minute technical deep-dive (separate invite if helpful). To reduce jetlag/personal-hours impact for your leaders across US/EU/APAC, I sketched an \"availability ladder\" (attached) — staggered start times that let each region join within normal working hours where possible, plus a mandatory 15-minute buffer window after the demo for quick follow-ups or exec exit.

Proposed candidate windows next week (local conversions included):
- Option A: 2028-05-18 07:00 PDT / 16:00 CEST / 23:00 JST (favored for US late morning / EU late afternoon; late for APAC)
- Option B: 2028-05-17 16:00 PDT / 01:00 CEST (+1 day) / 09:00 JST (APAC morning, EU very late)
- Option C: 2028-05-18 05:00 PDT / 14:00 CEST / 21:00 JST (early for US; still OK for EU/APAC)

I'm attaching an availability_matrix.xlsx with region columns and who-can-join ticks. Please mark preferences directly on that sheet or reply with preferred option(s). Note: EU had a DST adjustment earlier this spring — if there are specific calendar quirks for exec travel, let us know and we'll build fallback invites.

Attachments: demo_agenda_v2.pdf, availability_matrix.xlsx, calendar-buffer-template.ics

Next steps if we can agree a slot: I'll send a single canonical calendar invite with the buffer period as a separate calendar item (makes it clear for execs that their time is blocked but optional).

Thanks —
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference


From: Karen Liu <karen.liu@auroraai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rajiv Menon <rajiv@pi-analytics.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-05-14T17:48:00+02:00
Subject: Re: Aligning the demo: availability ladder + 15m buffer windows

Ben —

title: Renewal & expansion: anchoring concession + staged commit proposal
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Amelia Lopez <amelia@skyforge.io>, Jacob Park <jacob.park@skyforge.io>
Date: Thu, 16 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal & expansion: anchoring concession + staged commit proposal

Amelia / Jacob,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — appreciate the clear signal on wanting a multi-quarter price anchor while increasing committed capacity. Based on your current and forecasted usage (attached), here is a pragmatic path we can put on the table today to keep unit economics predictable for SkyForge while giving you headroom to scale: 

- Term: 12 months
- Commit: immediate uplift of 30% on current committed tokens, with 2 scheduled step-ups at month 4 and month 8 if usage thresholds are met (details in attachment).
- Discount: 10% off committed unit price for the first 6 months, reverting to list with a 3% loyalty band thereafter.
- Price hold: we can guarantee no list-price increases for 12 months on the committed tier; overages billed at standard pay-as-you-go.
- Burst credits: one-time 200k token credit to soften cutover in month 1.

Attachments: SkyForge_Monthly_Usage_Mar-Apr.csv (csv), Redwood_Amendment_Draft_v1.docx (docx)

If that looks directionally right, I'd like to schedule a short call to walk the operational handoffs (billing cadence, reporting) and prep a clean amendment/PO for your procurement. Proposed times: Thu 3pm PT or Fri 10am PT.

Goal: confirm an amendment this week so we can bind pricing and begin onboarding the burst pool next month.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference
phone: (415) 555-0147



From: Amelia Lopez <amelia@skyforge.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 16 May 2028 11:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal & expansion: anchoring concession + staged commit proposal

Ben — thanks for the quick proposal. A few notes from procurement and product ops after reviewing with Jacob: 

1) Discount: our procurement target is 12% on the committed tier (first 12 months), not just 6. We need visibility into unit economics for a full year to model our roadmap.
2) Price hold: we'd prefer an 18-month price hold for committed units. That helps us underwrite multi-quarter feature launches.
3) Commit cadence: the step-up schedule is fine but we need guaranteed availability/throughput SLAs during burst windows. Can you confirm how Redwood will prioritize capacity if we exceed commit during a launch?
4) PO: attaching our standard PO template so legal/procurement can speed things up once terms are agreed.

Attachment: SkyForge_PO_Template.pdf

Can we do Fri 10am PT for a short call? If we can get close on a 12%/18mo anchor then procurement can route a signed PO quickly.

—Amelia
Amelia Lopez
Head of Platform, SkyForge
amelia@skyforge.io



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Amelia Lopez <amelia@skyforge.io>, Jacob Park <jacob.park@skyforge.io>
Date: Thu, 16 May 2028 13:24:45 -0700
Subject: Fwd: SkyForge — procurement counter (price hold ask / discount request)

Looping you both in — SkyForge pushed back to 12% discount and wants an 18-month price hold on the committed tier. They sign fast if we can give them the anchor; procurement attached their PO template. Operational asks: they want SLA clarity for burst/launch windows.

My read / suggested response: 
- Move to a 9% discount for first 9 months (compromise), with a 15-month price hold on committed unit price.
- Keep the step-up commit schedule but add a short-form SLA addendum: guaranteed capacity allocation during scheduled launch windows (with pre-notification + a soft cap on concurrent burst concurrency) and a remuneration credit if we miss allocation (0.5% credit of monthly committed spend per missed SLA incident, capped).
- Provide one additional 100k token onboarding credit (so total burst credits = 300k).

Why this: preserves unit economics for Redwood vs 12%/18mo, gives SkyForge a multi-month anchor and more credits to smooth the migration. Finance: can you run quick P&L impact (delta vs baseline ARR) and confirm approval band? Legal: will the short SLA addendum require extended review?

Quoted from Amelia below for context.

On Thu, May 16, 2028 at 11:03 AM Amelia Lopez <amelia@skyforge.io> wrote:
> Ben — thanks for the quick proposal. A few notes from procurement and product ops after reviewing with Jacob:
> 
> 1) Discount: our procurement target is 12% on the committed tier (first 12 months), not just 6. We need visibility into unit economics for a full year to model our roadmap.
> 2) Price hold: we'd prefer an 18-month price hold for committed units. That helps us underwrite multi-quarter feature launches.
> 3) Commit cadence: the step-up schedule is fine but we need guaranteed availability/throughput SLAs during burst windows. Can you confirm how Redwood will prioritize capacity if we exceed commit during a launch?
> 4) PO: attaching our standard PO template so legal/procurement can speed things up once terms are agreed.
> 
> Attachment: SkyForge_PO_Template.pdf
> 
> Can we do Fri 10am PT for a short call? If we can get close on a 12%/18mo anchor then procurement can route a signed PO quickly.
> 
> —Amelia

Thanks — Ben



From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai>
To: Amelia Lopez <amelia@skyforge.io>, Jacob Park <jacob.park@skyforge.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 17 May 2028 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal & expansion: anchoring concession + staged commit proposal

Amelia / Jacob,

Appreciate the clarity and thanks Ben for the summary. We discussed internally and can move quickly to close this in the spirit of a partnership — below is the approved commercial compromise and a short ops/legal plan to make procurement comfortable: 

Commercial concession (approved):
- Term: 12 months (amendment replaces current annual arrangement)
- Commit: immediate +30% uplift with two step-ups at month 4 and month 8 tied to agreed usage thresholds (in the amendment).
- Discount: 9% off committed unit price for the first 9 months.
- Price hold: Redwood will hold committed-tier list pricing for 15 months from amendment effective date.
- Burst credits: 300k tokens total (200k onboarding + 100k additional).

Operational/legal plan:
- SLA addendum: short form that guarantees capacity prioritization for scheduled launches. If Redwood fails to meet a scheduled allocation, SkyForge will receive a credit equal to 0.5% of that month's committed spend per incident, capped at 2% per month.
- Procurement: we've accepted your PO template in principle; legal will insert the amendment and SLA addendum into a one-page exhibit (no material legal review expected).
- Next step: I uploaded an editable amendment (Redwood_Amendment_Draft_v1.docx) to the HubSpot record — please review and if the terms are OK we will route DocuSign. Link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/company/89234 and DocuSign pre-fill: https://docusign.net/sign/abcd-1234

If that alignment is acceptable, Ben — can you confirm Fri 10am PT for a 20-minute sync to walk the amendment and calendar the first scheduled launch window? Amelia, once you confirm the amendment language we'll push DocuSign immediately.

Regards,
Stephanie Nguyen
Head of Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
stephanie.nguyen@redwood.ai

title: Initial SOC 2 Type II intake — audit period & what to share
preview:
From: Laura Chen <laura.chen@greenpay.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: security@greenpay.com
Date: Tue, 15 May 2028 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: SOC 2 Type II — initial ask and audit period

Hi Ben —

We're kicking off vendor intake for GreenPay's upcoming audit and need Redwood's SOC 2 Type II materials and a short bridge letter confirming the audit period and any known coverage gaps. High-level asks:

title: Post-signature orchestration & MAP: VantageRx rollout, canary, migration, training
preview:
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Emily Park <emily.park@vantagerx.com>, procurement@vantagerx.com
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 May 2028 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Post-signature orchestration & MAP: VantageRx rollout, canary, migration, training

Hi Emily / Kevin / team,

First—congratulations again on signing. Attached is the initial Mutual Action Plan (MAP) workbook and a short evaluation plan that we put together based on our discussions. High-level ask: can you confirm a preferred kickoff week and an initial canary window? We propose a 2-week canary starting the week of May 22, with a staged migration thereafter.

Key items in the MAP (attached):
- Phase 0 (pre-kick): account provisioning, KMS onboarding, SOC2 evidence exchange — target complete by May 18
- Phase 1 (canary): 2-week canary (limited routes, 10% traffic) — proposed May 22–June 4
- Phase 2 (ramp): progressive traffic ramp and model tuning — targeted Jun 5–Jun 18
- Phase 3 (training & handoff): end-user training, ops runbook, SLA checkpoints — Jun 19–Jun 30

Immediate asks:
1) Confirm kickoff date (we penciled May 17 for a short alignment call).
2) Share procurement PO or invoicing contact for billing cadence.
3) Confirm whether you want the canary limited by route (recommended) or by region.

Attachments included: VantageRx_MAP_20280510.xlsx (MAP), EvaluationPlan_VRX_20280510.pdf (eval prompt set + success criteria), SOC2_Redwood_2027.pdf (requested security evidence).

We'll assign owners on our side—Vanessa will lead operations (canary gates, rollout), Marcus will lead the migration automation and infra validation. I'll coordinate the executive cadence and risk decisions.

Thanks,
Rishi Malhotra
Head of Sales Engineering
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: VantageRx_MAP_20280510.xlsx (spreadsheet)]
[Attachment: EvaluationPlan_VRX_20280510.pdf (pdf)]
[Attachment: SOC2_Redwood_2027.pdf (pdf)]

From: Emily Park <emily.park@vantagerx.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@vantagerx.com
Date: Fri, 11 May 2028 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-signature orchestration & MAP: VantageRx rollout, canary, migration, training

Hi Rishi—thanks and congrats all around.

We're aligned on the proposed phases. Quick responses:
- Kickoff: May 17 works for a 45-minute alignment call (please send invite).
- Canary: prefer route-limited canary (we have a high-volume claims route we want excluded during canary).
- Security evidence: SOC2 is fine, but procurement will need a slightly longer retention policy summary and an answer on data residency (we're US-only).

Procurement note: I've looped Kevin Li (cc'd) and our procurement alias. He'll confirm PO timing and PO number.

Also: for the EvaluationPlan, can you confirm whether the eval harness will run against production-sim traffic or a mirrored stream? We prefer mirrored if possible.

Looking forward—EM

Emily Park
Director, Platform Engineering | VantageRx
em: emily.park@vantagerx.com | t: (415) 555-0198

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Emily Park <emily.park@vantagerx.com>, Kevin Li <kevin.li@vantagerx.com>, procurement@vantagerx.com
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 12 May 2028 08:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-signature orchestration & MAP: VantageRx rollout, canary, migration, training

Emily—thanks. A few clarifications and concrete owners below.

Evaluation harness: we recommend mirrored traffic for the initial canary to avoid impacting live users; Redwood's runtime supports stream mirroring with anonymization hooks. The EvaluationPlan (attached) includes the prompt set, pass/fail thresholds (latency p95 < 450ms, error rate < 0.5%), and manual QA checkpoints for subjective quality.

Owners (Redwood):
- Vanessa Ortiz: Canary gates, rollback/runbook owner (vanessa@redwood.com)
- Marcus Lin: Infra validation, VPC/KMS onboarding, automation scripts (marcus@redwood.com)
- Rishi Malhotra: Exec updates, change approvals, escalation point (rishi@redwood.com)

Proposed week-by-week MAP milestones (excerpt from workbook):
- May 17: Kickoff (alignment call) — confirm routes in scope, PO status, DocuSign MSA signature
- May 18–21: Provisioning & keys — VPC peering, KMS key import, whitelist IPs
- May 22–Jun 4: Canary (mirrored traffic on non-claims routes) — daily health reports, canary gates on token cost and latency
- Jun 5–Jun 18: Progressive ramp to 60% traffic — model tuning windows and regression checks
- Jun 19–Jun 30: Training sessions (ops + developer) and SLA handover; final acceptance sign-off

We're adding one additional deliverable to the MAP: a short runbook named "VRX-Canary-Gates.md" that enumerates automatic rollback triggers. That will be posted to the shared drive after kickoff.

I've attached the MAP workbook and EvaluationPlan again for easy reference.

Thanks—I'll schedule the May 17 kickoff for 9:30am PT. Invite to follow.

Rishi

[Attachment: VantageRx_MAP_20280510.xlsx (spreadsheet)]
[Attachment: EvaluationPlan_VRX_20280510.pdf (pdf)]

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Emily Park <emily.park@vantagerx.com>
Date: Mon, 13 May 2028 11:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-signature orchestration & MAP: VantageRx rollout, canary, migration, training

Looping in a few operational checklist items and quick questions so procurement and platform teams have visibility.

Operational checklist (short):
- Confirm IP allowlist and CIDR blocks for peering by May 18
- KMS key exchange and asymmetric key wrap test (Marcus to run)
- Canary traffic filter: provide exact route patterns to include/exclude (Emily to confirm by May 16)
- Monitoring: we'll provide Grafana dashboard link + runbook alerts (thresholds in MAP)
- Backout plan: immediate fallback to smaller model family + rate-limit reduction (documented in VRX-Canary-Gates.md)

Questions for VantageRx:
1) Data retention policy for logs: do you require logs to be retained >90 days? If so we'll create a scoped ingest policy and map that to your retention SLA.
2) Will you require a private endpoint (VPC) only or are there any public-facing routes?

On quoting the evaluation point—mirrored traffic is the safest path. For the canary let's plan 1 week of mirror + 1 week of low-percentage live routing, if that aligns with Emily's request.

Thanks—Vanessa

Vanessa Ortiz
Principal Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

title: QuantumBridge: sudden throughput/500 spike — proposed mitigations and schedule
preview:
From: Samantha Reed <samantha@quantumbridge.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Cc: Amit Patel <amit.patel@quantumbridge.io>
Date: Mon, 14 May 2028 13:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production degradation - latency & 5xx surge

Ben —

We're seeing a rapid increase in p99 latency (from ~180ms to >1.6s) and a spike in 500/502 responses starting ~11:50 PDT this morning. Throughput for our main routing key increased ~3x which coincided with numerous token-level retries from our side. Our SREs captured a tail of many KV eviction events and significant queueing on the inference nodes. This is impacting three critical customer flows and we've had session failures in the storefront checkout path.

Can Redwood provide immediate mitigation and a timeline for a fix? We need an ETA for restoring p99 under 400ms and eliminating the 5xx burst.

We've attached a short snapshot (throughput-snapshot-20280514.png) and a small log excerpt (kv-eviction-tail.log) showing eviction timestamps. Happy to jump on a call.

—Samantha Reed
Head of Platform Reliability
QuantumBridge



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Reed <samantha@quantumbridge.io>
Cc: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 May 2028 13:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Production degradation - latency & 5xx surge

Samantha — thanks for the data and the heads-up. I’m sorry you're seeing this. I’ve looped in runtime (Markus), capacity (Grace) and customer ops (Laura).

Immediate next steps:
- We’ll triage logs and telemetry to confirm whether this is HOL/blocking vs. eviction-induced miss storms.
- If it’s hot-lane overload we can temporarily bump hot-lane capacity for your tenant and enable a conservative eviction cooldown to reduce churn.
- I’ll propose a short mitigation plan and call time in the next 20 mins.

On my side I’ll also open an incident and track with you directly.

—Ben
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Reed <samantha@quantumbridge.io>
Date: Mon, 14 May 2028 13:30:15 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Production degradation - latency & 5xx surge

Forwarding Samantha's note — TL;DR: p99 >1.6s, 5xx spike, eviction log attached. QuantumBridge says throughput jumped ~3x starting ~11:50.

Ask for runtime: quick hypothesis and surgical mitigation we can apply without a long deploy. Customer requires p99 <400ms ASAP.

Background/context (internal):
- Tenant is on Dedicated pool D-us-west-2-b with default eviction cooldown 30s and hot-lane capacity of 4 slots.
- Model: redw-3.1-ggml (quantized) with typical seq len 512 for this customer.

Markus/Grace — can you confirm if a short-term change (increase hot-lane to 10, extend cooldown to 120s, or route a % of traffic to a compatible variant) will be safe to apply and what the rollback steps are? Put any quick runbook links or exact CLI commands in reply.

—Ben

From: Markus Klein <markus@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samantha Reed <samantha@quantumbridge.io>
Date: Mon, 14 May 2028 13:46:02 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Production degradation - latency & 5xx surge

Ben / team / Samantha — quick triage notes after looking at the logs and recent traces (attached kv-eviction-tail.log):

What I see: repeated prefix-evictions from the same hot keys that then trigger synchronous backend cache-fill operations. That creates short-lived HOL (head-of-line) blocking inside the scheduler: the lane holding the request waits on a KV fill which delays many short requests behind it. The behavior is amplified by the eviction cooldown being too short (30s) relative to the request burst pattern; the cache churn creates an eviction spiral.

Immediate mitigations I recommend (order matters):
1) Apply eviction cooldown extension to 120s for the tenant (reduces churn).
2) Increase hot-lane capacity from 4 -> 9 for the affected pool to reduce queueing on hot keys.
3) Enable conservative prefix caching (pin top-50 keys for 15m) for the next 3 hours while we stabilize.
4) If still degraded, route 10% of requests to a slightly lower latency model variant (redw-3.1-ggml-fp16) as graceful degradation to avoid 5xx cascades.

Safety notes / rollback:
- All changes are runtime flags that do not require model reloading. Rollback is immediate via the control plane (eviction cooldown back to 30s; hot-lane back to 4).
- I attached a quick runbook (runbook-temp-routing.md) with exact commands and the monitoring checks to watch (queue depth, eviction rate, p99).

I can run the changes now if we have customer approval for a brief disruption window (expected < 2 mins to apply, with immediate visibility).

—Markus
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Attachment: kv-eviction-tail.log (excerpt)

- Apply eviction cooldown -> 120s
- Increase hot-lane capacity to 9
- Pin top-50 prefix keys for 15m
- If needed, route 10% traffic to redw-3.1-ggml-fp16

Proposed timeline: apply changes within 5 minutes of approval; monitor for 30 minutes; reconvene for follow-up.

Action requested: approve immediate apply or request call time.

—Ben

Hi Sarah / team,

Thanks again for the productive renewal call last week. Per our conversation, we're preparing paperwork to: 
- shift from a 12mo to an 18mo term (aligned with your fiscal cadence)
- add a phased capacity uplift: +20% immediate, +30% at month 6, +50% at month 12 (see SOW draft)

Attached are the current SOW (Infrapoint_SOW_v3.docx) and the blank order form (Redwood_OrderForm_v5.pdf). Before we loop Legal, wanted to confirm two operational points so we minimize back-and-forth: 
1) Bill run: do you want the uplift invoiced pro rata at each stage or consolidated at month 12?
2) Term effective date: are you expecting the 18mo term to start on 2028-07-01 or the signature date?

If those align, I'll ask Legal to prepare a limited MSA amendment (term swap + ordering schedule) and we'll circulate redlines. Goal: sign by end of May so onboarding can start July 1.

Avery

title: Coordinating cross-prospect product walkthrough + prep materials
Re: Coupa invite stuck in SSO redirect / proxy handshake
preview:
From: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@acmelogix.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-05-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Can't accept Coupa invite — stuck on SSO redirect

Hi Ben,

We received the supplier invite from Redwood/Coupa this morning but when I click the activation link I get stuck in a redirect loop that keeps bouncing between our corporate SSO and the Coupa auth page. The page briefly flashes “Redirecting to identity provider…” and then returns to the Redwood/Coupa login. Tried Chrome and Edge, same result.

We are behind a corporate proxy (Zscaler) that rewrites some headers — I can attach a screenshot of the browser state (attached). We need to get set up to receive POs — can you help reissue or advise a workaround?

Thanks,
Sarah Lee
Senior Procurement Ops
Acme Logix
[screenshot-redirect.png attached]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@acmelogix.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-05-15T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Can't accept Coupa invite — stuck on SSO redirect

Hi Sarah — thanks for the detail and screenshot, that helps. I've looped in our identity/infra lead (Karthik) and Neha from our account team to help triage.

Two quick asks while we dig in:
- Can you confirm whether an incognito window produced the same loop?
- Which browser and version are you on (you mentioned Chrome/Edge — exact versions if possible).

I'll open an internal ops ticket and attach your screenshot and headers. In the meantime attaching a short troubleshooting checklist that often helps for proxy-related SSO loops (portal-troubleshooting-steps.pdf). If that doesn't work we'll reissue an invite with a temporary bypass link.

Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0191
[portal-troubleshooting-steps.pdf attached]

On Tue, May 15, 2028 at 09:12 AM Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@acmelogix.com> wrote:
> Hi Ben,
> 
> We received the supplier invite from Redwood/Coupa this morning but when I click the activation link I get stuck in a redirect loop ...

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@acmelogix.com>
Date: 2028-05-15T10:28:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Can't accept Coupa invite — proxy rewrites?

Karthik — forwarding Sarah's note. They are an urgent onboarding for a mid-market PO flow (expecting first PO tomorrow). Customer reports Zscaler/proxy rewriting headers which sometimes breaks our SSO assertion flow.

Relevant context:
- Customer tenant: acmelogix.coupa.com
- Customer saw: repeated redirect between idp.acmelogix.com and auth.coupa.com
- Attached screenshot: shows ephemeral query param token present then disappears after redirect.

Can infra check whether we are validating the presence/ordering of X-Forwarded-For and X-Forwarded-Proto in the redirect handshake? If we need a transient relax of strict header ordering we should patch ASAP.

Neha
Neha Kapoor
Account Manager, Redwood Inference

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@acmelogix.com>
Date: 2028-05-15T11:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Can't accept Coupa invite — stuck on SSO redirect

Thanks for the ping + details. Quick summary of what I checked and the fix we pushed: 

Investigation notes:
- Replayed the customer's redirect sequence from our staging logs. Observed that when the proxy strips the pfv= param on the first hop our session cookie validator rejects the inbound assertion and sends a redirect back to the IdP. That creates the loop.
- We also saw X-Forwarded-Host arriving as a comma-separated value (Zscaler adds the original host + proxy host). Our validator was doing an exact match on host header ordering.

Action taken (transient patch):
1) Relaxed host-header ordering check for provider callbacks for the acmelogix tenant.
2) Added tolerant parsing for X-Forwarded-Host and X-Forwarded-Proto with a 2-hour flag (ops change RI-8421).
3) Reissued a fresh invite link that bypasses the normal redirect TTL so they can complete enrollment: https://coupa.com/invite/REISSUE-7b9f (expires in 4 hours).

Customer-facing instructions (please follow exactly):
- Clear browser cookies for acmelogix.coupa.com and idp.acmelogix.com.
- Open an incognito/private window.
- Use this reissue link (attached in coupa-invite-reissue-link.txt) and complete SSO flow.

I've filed RI-8421 for the long-term fix to normalize forwarded header parsing across tenants. Ben, Neha — I left the patch in a transient state; we'll watch metrics for 2 hours and roll to prod if stable.

Karthik Iyer
Staff Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference

[log excerpt below]
--- begin excerpt ---
2028-05-15T11:18:23Z inbound: GET /sso/callback?token=eyJ.. pfv=hdr1
2028-05-15T11:18:23Z headers: Host: acmelogix.coupa.com
2028-05-15T11:18:23Z X-Forwarded-Host: idp.acmelogix.com, zscaler-gw.internal
2028-05-15T11:18:23Z cookie: session=expired
--- end excerpt ---

From: Sarah Lee <sarah.lee@acmelogix.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-05-15T14:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Can't accept Coupa invite — stuck on SSO redirect

Ben / Karthik / Neha — thanks, that did it. I cleared cookies, used an incognito window, and the reissued invite completed the SSO handshake. I can see the supplier dashboard and the test PO came through.

Appreciate the quick turn — please keep RI-8421 on the backlog for a permanent fix; we'll want to onboard a couple more Acme sub-tenants later this quarter.

Best,
Sarah
Sarah Lee
Senior Procurement Ops
Acme Logix
[screenshot-redirect.png previously attached]

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. VectorLane is ready to move to a joint technical validation so our teams can sign off on the connector and plan a Q3 joint launch. High-level asks from our side:

- Confirm field mappings and auth modes (we support OAuth2 + API keys).
- Run an interoperability test with our staging dataset (PII-masked).
- Validate retry/backpressure behavior under burst loads.
- Exchange basic compliance evidence (SOC2 summary + data flow diagram).

I've attached our integration spec (v1) and proposed test matrix. Can your team run a 2-week validation sprint starting May 21? If not, propose alternatives.

Best,
Amit

1) Marissa to draft a mutual test plan (shared workbook) by EOD 5/15.
