Hi Sam / Kylie —

Thanks again for taking the time today — great conversation and a clear signal that CommercialPilot has a pragmatic production use case for automated contract summarization and clause extraction. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum: 

1) Model fit & benchmarks (short list):
   - Candidate models to evaluate: redwood-llama-13b-chat-qat, mistral-instruct-7b, and redwood-quant-13b (FP16+8-bit expermental).
   - Benchmarks we plan to run: extraction precision/recall, hallucination rate on labeled clauses, and 99th-percentile latency on a 1k concurrent throughput profile.

2) Prompt / sample data request: 
   - Can you share a seed set of ~75–125 real user prompts/examples (examples of: \"summarize clause X\", \"extract payment terms\", and a handful of noisy OCR cases)? CSV is perfect (one prompt per row).
   - If any prompts include PII/PHI, please flag them or replace with <REDACTED>. If you prefer, send a separate file called sensitive_prompts.csv and we will treat it with stricter handling.

3) Redaction guidance & edge cases:
   - We typically ask customers to: (a) mark tokens that must be removed, (b) provide examples of heavily redacted outputs you accept, and (c) flag any legalese you want preserved verbatim.

4) Next milestones & timing:
   - You send seed prompts by Tue 2028-04-24.
   - We run an initial model-fit sweep and return top-line results + a short eval deck by Thu 2028-04-27.

Attachments: demo_highlights_0420.mp4 (clips from today), seed_prompts_20280420.csv (example template).

If that plan looks right I'll open a HubSpot task and assign an internal owner to the prompt ingestion.

Thanks — looking forward to the seed prompts.

—
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha_rahman@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0187

- On redaction: for now we prefer that your process redacts account numbers and SSNs. Date ranges and currency amounts can stay if they're critical to the extract.

Subject: Internal: CommercialPilot — eval plan & ingestion checklist

Aisha / team — plan to own the ingestion pipeline for CP. Notes / asks: 

- Prompt ingestion format: prefer CSV with columns: prompt_id, prompt_text, source_type (support/email/ocr), redaction_flag (yes/no), notes. Use seed_prompts_20280420.csv as template.
- If CP sends original PDFs for OCR edge cases, we will store them in a restricted bucket and run an OCR + noisy-token-normalization pass prior to evaluation. For first sweep text snippets suffice — ask them to include at least 20 noisy OCR samples.
- Evaluation harness: we'll run three model candidates with the following metrics: extraction F1, hallucination rate (manual sample), normalized latency @ p99, and token cost per 1k requests. I'll attach the draft eval-plan.md (attached).
- Model recommendations: start with redwood-llama-13b-chat-qat (best balance), fall back to mistral-instruct-7b for faster, cheaper runs. Try 8-bit quantized variant for cost tests.
- Ops: propose a 48-hour canary in Dedicated with kv-cache enabled and a small persistent cache for repeated prompts. Karthik, can you spin a canary image and get perf numbers?

I'll draft the HubSpot task and the evaluation deck outline tonight.

—Ben

title: EU1: Staggered service refresh — opt-in & blackout windows
NorthPeak: Incident summary, timeline, and actionable commitments
preview:
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Jones <alex.jones@northpeak.com>, Sarah Chen <sarah.chen@northpeak.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Apr 2028 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: NorthPeak: Incident summary, timeline, and actionable commitments

Alex / Sarah —

Thanks again for your patience while we completed the technical review of Friday’s availability degradation that impacted inference requests routed to your dedicated pool. Below is a concise customer-facing summary; we’ve attached a short RCA PDF and the raw event timeline for your verification.

Summary (TL;DR)
- Impact: 2028-04-14 21:12 UTC — 2028-04-14 21:46 UTC: elevated token latency and ~7% client error rate for inference calls for a subset of NorthPeak traffic (peak effect: 21:20–21:28 UTC).
- Root cause (short): a mis-configured KV-cache eviction threshold deployed as part of a performance tuning rollout caused aggressive prefix eviction under a short burst of wide-context traffic, which triggered excessive origin rehydration and amplification of backend load. Autoscaler decisions briefly lagged, increasing tail latency.
- Customer-facing actions completed: immediate rollback of the rollout (21:30 UTC), targeted cache policy override for your pool, and a hotfix to autoscaler grace-window logic (22:10 UTC).

Attached materials: northpeak_rca_20280415.pdf (summary), timeline-0415.csv (event timestamps), kv-eviction-log-0415.txt (filtered logs).

Immediate next steps we own (high-level)

2) Medium-term (by 2028-05-02): add telemetry for eviction-rate-by-prefix and an alert that triggers before client-visible error rate increases.
3) Process change (by 2028-05-15): require targeted traffic replay and a rollout kill-switch test before any eviction-threshold change reaches production.

From: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Jones <alex.jones@northpeak.com>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 16 Apr 2028 10:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: NorthPeak: Incident summary, timeline, and actionable commitments

Alex —

Thanks for the questions. I’m Marcus (Infra Engineering, platform serving), following up with the technical timeline and a precise RCA for your audit folks. I’ve included the full set of logs as attachments in our internal bundle (we've shared the filtered excerpt with you; we can provide additional slices under an NDA if required). Below are the facts we validated.

Full technical timeline (UTC)
- 2028-04-14T21:10:54Z: Scheduled rollout job (eviction-tuning v2.03) started; target subset: 12 pools (includes NorthPeak pool NP-svc-12).
- 2028-04-14T21:18:03Z: Observed burst of wide-context sessions from a small set of clients; unique prefix fanout increased by ~320%.
- 2028-04-14T21:19:10Z: Eviction controller (new threshold) began evicting aggressively; local cache miss rate increased, rehydration calls to origin increased 12x for NP-svc-12.
- 2028-04-14T21:20:45Z: Upstream dataplane CPU climbed; average token latency exceeded 1.6s for affected calls. Error rate (5xx) rose to ~7% for a 4-minute window.
- 2028-04-14T21:30:12Z: Operations rolled back v2.03 for NP-svc-12 and applied targeted override to restore prior eviction policy.
- 2028-04-14T21:35:40Z: Eviction miss rate began trending back to baseline; rehydration calls returned to normal.
- 2028-04-14T22:09:58Z: Autoscaler hotfix deployed (shorter scale-up window), preventing delayed scaling in future short bursts.

Root cause analysis (detail)
- Primary: a regression in the eviction controller’s new governor logic where a percentile-based cap was incorrectly computed for pools with low observed prefix reuse; the math treated an empty sample as zero and then propagated an overly permissive eviction target. Under bursty, high-fanout sessions the controller evicted prefixes that should have been retained.
- Secondary: rollout controls lacked a traffic-shape preflight check (we rollouts by pool count, not by traffic shape). This allowed the change to hit a pool experiencing an uncommon traffic profile which magnified impact.
- Tertiary (amplifier): autoscaler grace-time configuration deferred scale-up during the initial minutes of load spike. Because the eviction issue increased origin load, the delayed scaling extended the user-visible latency window.

Customer data integrity
- No customer data was deleted or truncated. Affected calls either timed out or returned 5xx errors; successful responses are intact. We confirmed no embedding outputs were silently dropped from persistent storage.

What we changed immediately (already in prod)
- Rollback of eviction-tuning v2.03 for NP-svc-12 (rolled back 21:30 UTC).
- Pool-level override to restore prior eviction thresholds (21:32 UTC).
- Autoscaler hotfix to reduce scale-up evaluation window (22:09 UTC).

Planned corrective actions and timelines
- By 2028-04-18 (complete): add an eviction-rate alert (pre-customer-impact threshold) and automated must-pass synthetic for any eviction-policy rollout.
- By 2028-05-02: require traffic-shape sampling as part of rollout gating; integrate with the canary runner so pools with atypical fanout are excluded by default.
- By 2028-05-15: run a cross-team war-room drill for rollout kill-switches and verify the rollback path within sub-10 minutes for any pool.

Verification for your team
- We will provide a signed CSV of the event window (timeline-0415.csv) and a sanitized set of logs that include correlation IDs for your request samples. If you need the raw logs in full, we can share under the existing DPA/NDA process.

SLA credits
- We will calculate credits against the canonical window you confirm. Preliminary calculation indicates a single-incident credit equivalent to 0.25% of monthly committed usage for NP-svc-12; procurement and billing will finalize once you confirm the official window.

If you want, we can do a 30-minute technical walkthrough tomorrow at 10:30 UTC and include our incident runbook, the remediation checklist, and a short demo of the canary validation we’ll run.

Regards,
Marcus Lin
Senior Engineer, Inference Infrastructure — Redwood Inference
marcus@redwood.ai

title: Proposal discussion: Uptime/Response packs and escalation design for PioneerAI
preview:
From: Alex Hoffman <alex.h@pioneerai.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@pioneerai.com
Date: Tue, 10 Apr 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Commercial proposal: Uptime & response options for PioneerAI

Hi Ben,

Thanks again for the product demo last week — the team was impressed with the latency profiles and the routing/fallback story. Procurement asked me to get a clearer picture of the commercial options for premium support: specifically uptime tiers (99.95 vs 99.99), guaranteed response times for Sev1/Sev2, and how escalation coverage (oncall staffing) is priced.

High level constraints: PioneerAI is evaluating an annual committed spend with Redwood (targeting $1.2M ARR) and wants to know what tradeoffs are available between higher uptime SLAs and credits / seat-based retainer pricing. Also, we operate a global platform but will start with a US region deployment.

Could you send a concise options matrix and any standard contract language that defines credits and measurement windows? Procurement also asked for an SOW template and a sample escalation roster.

Thanks,
Alex

--
Alex Hoffman
Head of Infrastructure, PioneerAI
alex.h@pioneerai.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Hoffman <alex.h@pioneerai.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, procurement@pioneerai.com
Date: Wed, 11 Apr 2028 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Commercial proposal: Uptime & response options for PioneerAI

Hi Alex — appreciate the clarity. Attaching a short options matrix and a draft SOW for your procurement team to review (see attached: Pioneer_SOW_v2.pdf, uptime-credit-matrix.xlsx). Below is the distilled set of options; I tried to map to your $1.2M ARR target so we can show realistic discounts at commit levels.

Option A — Standard Assurance (no retainer)
- SLA: 99.95% monthly uptime
- P1 response (business hours): 1 hour initial triage, 4 hour remediation target
- P2 response: 4 hour triage, 48 hour remediation target
- Credits: sliding scale up to 5% monthly credit for sustained outages
- Pricing: pay-as-you-go; base token pricing applies

Option B — Priority Assurance (recommended for production)
- SLA: 99.99% monthly uptime
- P1 response (24/7): 30 minute initial triage, 8 hour remediation target
- P2 response (24/7): 1 hour triage, 24 hour remediation target
- Escalation: named escalation path + bi-weekly oncall overlap for first 90 days
- Credits: higher cap, faster credit triggers; up to 10% monthly credit in rare cases
- Pricing: annual commit with 15% discount on unit price + optional oncall retainer

Option C — Premium Assurance (enterprise retainer)
- SLA: 99.995% (zonal failover included)
- P1 response (24/7): 15 minute triage, 4 hour remediation SLO
- Dedicated oncall rotation and guaranteed engineer response within 30 minutes
- Credits: bespoke, tiered; SLA penalties limited by capped credits per contract
- Pricing: reserved capacity + retainer; volume-based discounting available at your $1.2M target

Tradeoffs and notes:
- Commitment materially reduces unit price and can justify an oncall retainer reduction.
- Quantization/caching profiles can reduce token spend which changes the economics of higher SLA tiers.
- For any Priority/Premium package we include an onboarding week with runbooks and a shared escalation roster (sample included).

Next steps I suggest: 1) procurement reviews SOW; 2) we agree on target uptime tier (B or C); 3) we draft DocuSign with the chosen retainer/credit matrix. If helpful I can also produce a blended example showing monthly credits under different outage scenarios.

Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Alex Hoffman <alex.h@pioneerai.com>
Cc: Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.ai>, procurement@pioneerai.com
Date: Thu, 12 Apr 2028 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Commercial proposal: Uptime & response options for PioneerAI

Adding a couple technical/ops points from the oncall & SRE side to make sure expectations are aligned:

- Measurement window: we measure uptime on a monthly rolling window with region-specific sensors. Zonal failover in Premium Assurance includes automated routing to warm nodes; note that failover time often dominates the perceived outage duration.
- Credits calculation: credits are based on normalized request-weighted downtime (not raw node time) to reflect production impact. We include an example calc in uptime-credit-matrix.xlsx.
- Escalation roster: for Priority Assurance we provide 24/7 oncall with a named backup; for Premium we provide a dedicated rotation aligned to your peak hours.
- Runbook ownership: we expect PioneerAI to maintain a short runbook for basic playbooks; Redwood will own higher-level incident orchestration.

I'll also highlight the onboarding-week deliverables inside the SOW: runbook handoff, pager routing, and a 90-day stabilization review.

Grace

Subject: Re: Commercial proposal: Uptime & response options for PioneerAI

Team — thanks for the clear options. Pioneer procurement has a couple of asks and a counter-proposal:

Counter-proposal highlights:
- Target: Priority Assurance (Option B) with a twist — Pioneer requests a 12% volume discount at $1.2M ARR and waiver of the first-month retainer (if retainer chosen).
- SLA floor: we require explicit measurement windows & the right to an independent verification once per year (limited to 1 audit).
- Credits cap: we request that monthly credits not be limited to an amount that would make remediation incentives negligible; asking for a min 8% cap for persistent degradations beyond 72 hours.
- DocuSign: please send a redlined SOW and a DocuSign link for the commercial terms; we prefer a single DocuSign with SOW + MSA.

Attached: Pioneer_SOW_v2.pdf (redlines), sample PO terms inlined. We also expect an onboarding kickoff within two weeks of signature.

Pioneer Procurement

Alex / Procurement — thanks for the counter-proposal and the redlines. Quick replies to your asks: 

- Discount: we can do 12% at $1.2M ARR if Pioneer commits to annual billing and a 9 month minimum notice period for capacity true-ups. I'll mark the discount in the commercial sheet and reflect it in the DocuSign.
- Retainer waiver: we can waive the first-month retainer for Priority Assurance as requested.
- Audit: happy to include a single independent verification per year with mutually agreed scope and cost-sharing for the audit (we'll propose a vendor list).
- Credits cap & calculation: we will accept the 8% minimum cap for persistent degradations beyond 72 hours; we'll keep the normalized request-weighted calculation but will include a worked example in the DocuSign attachments.

Next steps: 1) I will upload a DocuSign package with the redlined SOW + commercial sheet + MSA by EOD tomorrow; 2) once countersigned we will schedule the onboarding kickoff (we can meet week of 4/29).

- Pricing: pay-as-you-go; base token pricing applies

Option B — Priority Assurance (recommended for production)
- SLA: 99.99% monthly uptime
- P1 response (24/7): 30 minute initial triage, 8 hour remediation target
- P2 response (24/7): 1 hour triage, 24 hour remediation target
- Escalation: named escalation path + bi-weekly oncall overlap for first 90 days
- Credits: higher cap, faster credit triggers; up to 10% monthly credit in rare cases
- Pricing: annual commit with 15% discount on unit price + optional oncall retainer

Option C — Premium Assurance (enterprise retainer)
- SLA: 99.995% (zonal failover included)
- P1 response (24/7): 15 minute triage, 4 hour remediation SLO
- Dedicated oncall rotation and guaranteed engineer response within 30 minutes
- Credits: bespoke, tiered; SLA penalties limited by capped credits per contract
- Pricing: reserved capacity + retainer; volume-based discounting available at your $1.2M target

Tradeoffs and notes:
- Commitment materially reduces unit price and can justify an oncall retainer reduction.
- Quantization/caching profiles can reduce token spend which changes the economics of higher SLA tiers.
- For any Priority/Premium package we include an onboarding week with runbooks and a shared escalation roster (sample included).

Next steps I suggest: 1) procurement reviews SOW; 2) we agree on target uptime tier (B or C); 3) we draft DocuSign with the chosen retainer/credit matrix. If helpful I can also produce a blended example showing monthly credits under different outage scenarios.

Ben

--
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

Adding a couple technical/ops points from the oncall & SRE side to make sure expectations are aligned:

- Measurement window: we measure uptime on a monthly rolling window with region-specific sensors. Zonal failover in Premium Assurance includes automated routing to warm nodes; note that failover time often dominates the perceived outage duration.
- Credits calculation: credits are based on normalized request-weighted downtime (not raw node time) to reflect production impact. We include an example calc in uptime-credit-matrix.xlsx.
- Escalation roster: for Priority Assurance we provide 24/7 oncall with a named backup; for Premium we provide a dedicated rotation aligned to your peak hours.
- Runbook ownership: we expect PioneerAI to maintain a short runbook for basic playbooks; Redwood will own higher-level incident orchestration.

I'll also highlight the onboarding-week deliverables inside the SOW: runbook handoff, pager routing, and a 90-day stabilization review.

- MedSync will send de-identified patient metadata and customer-provided messages for routing/triage (PII surface depends on routing rules).

3) Retention: please confirm target retention for raw inputs and derived artifacts. We assume 90 days for tokens by default unless you require longer.
4) Breach notification: what is your internal SLA for notification (we typically commit to notify customers without undue delay; for sensitive health workloads we can discuss a 48-72 hour window).
5) Subprocessors: we’ll provide our current subprocessor registry and can deliver a CSV with locations and purposes. Please confirm any blocked subprocessors.

Deliverables we will attach in this thread: Redwood DPA snapshot (summary), current subprocessor register, and a sample audit-log extract for the last 7 days (token-level redacted).

Subject: Re: Post-call: privacy qualification and evidence request

Hi Julie / MedSync Legal team,

I’m Markus from Redwood Legal — looping in to respond to the legal / breach / subprocessor items. Short answers and attachments below.

1) Controller vs. Processor: Per our standard operating model, Redwood will act as processor with respect to customer-provided PHI and customer data you send to the service. Any product telemetry (usage metrics that are not customer data) is treated under our internal analytics policy; we will not use customer-provided PHI for product training without explicit, written opt-in. We can add that language to the DPA.

2) Subprocessors: attached is our current subprocessor register (redwood_subprocessors_register.csv). It includes name, service provided, and primary processing region. If you identify any subprocessors that require additional contractual safeguards, flag them and we’ll handle on a case-by-case basis.

3) Breach notification: Redwood’s standard commitment is to notify customers without undue delay and to provide preliminary facts within 48 hours of confirmation of an incident affecting customer data. We can discuss contractual language for a 72-hour maximum notification window if that is your preference; operationally we aim for 48 hours preliminary notice and a follow-up remediation timeline. Please confirm whether MedSync needs the explicit 72-hour clause or if the 48-hour preliminary notice + ongoing updates is acceptable.

4) Evidence & logs: we can produce token-level redacted audit exports for the requested 7-day window (see attached sample audit-log-sample-7days.csv). The export is redacted for PHI at rest and shows event timestamps, route, model version, and the audit token hash. For contractual evidence, we can also provide SOC2-related artifacts under an NDA or via your security portal.

5) Security contact: Primary contact for incident response at Redwood is: Dev Patel, Security Lead — dev.patel@redwood.ai, +1 415-555-0188. For escalations please include security@redwood.ai and legal@redwood.ai.

Next steps: 
- Ava will circulate the standard Redwood DPA (we’ve attached a snapshot).
- If MedSync wants explicit 72-hour maximum notification, confirm in reply and we’ll mark that clause for review with our contracts team.
- If you want specific subprocessor exclusions, send names and we will evaluate.

Regards,
Markus Klein
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
markus.klein@redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood_subprocessors_register.csv, audit-log-sample-7days.csv

title: Security mileposts & data retention items for MAP - Pinecrest
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Sam Patel <sam.patel@pinecrest.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Apr 2028 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: MAP draft & security mileposts (SOC 2 / SIG / DPA / retention)

Hi Sam —

Thanks again for the time yesterday. As promised, I attached an initial mutual action plan (MAP_Pinecrest_v1.pdf) focused on the evaluation and close timeline. Quick highlights from our side you’ll see in the MAP:

- Security gating milestones (SOC 2 evidence checkpoint, SIG/CAIQ review, DPA redline acceptance).
- Data retention and deletion options (30/90/365 day retention windows, export tooling).
- Proposed acceptance criteria for a production sign-off (SOC 2 in-scope evidence + signed DPA).
- Target close window: 8–10 weeks from kick-off assuming SIG answers within 5 business days.

Can you confirm the internal owners for legal and infosec on your side? I put Jill Moreno as the primary contact for security in the draft but happy to update. Also flagging a few items where we expect questions:

1) Whether Pinecrest requires SOC 2 Type II report or attestation for a specified period.
2) Any mandatory CLA or additional vendor questionnaires beyond SIG.
3) Your preferred retention policy option and whether we should scope a short-term exception for the POC.

Next steps: if this MAP looks like a reasonable starting point, can we lock in a 30-minute alignment call tomorrow to walk the timeline and owners? I’ll bring Karthik (commercial) and Marissa (solutions/security lead).

Thanks —

Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachment: MAP_Pinecrest_v1.pdf (MAP with milestone dates and acceptance criteria)

From: Pinecrest Security Team <security@pinecrest.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Sam Patel <sam.patel@pinecrest.com>, Jill Moreno <jill.moreno@pinecrest.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Apr 2028 13:17:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP draft & security mileposts (SOC 2 / SIG / DPA / retention)

Ben —

Thanks for sending the MAP. Quick responses inline:

- Owners: Jill (Infosec) and I (Sam) will own the evaluation on our side. Legal will be represented by our counsel (to be looped for DPA).
- SOC 2: We require SOC 2 Type II covering the most recent 12 months for production; for POC we can accept a SOC 2 Type I plus an attestation letter, depending on risk. Please indicate what evidence you can surface within 2 weeks.
- SIG: We’ll run through the SIG/CAIQ spreadsheet and return answers. I attached the blank we prefer to use.
- Retention: Pinecrest prefers a 90-day default retention for POC data, with a 365-day option for production. We need API-based deletion and an export (csv/json) within 72 hours.

A few clarifying questions:
1) For the DPA redline, is Redwood open to a mutual indemnity clause for data breaches tied to Redwood’s SOC 2 findings?
2) What is Redwood’s current default data center region for Pinecrest customers? (we’d prefer us-east-1)

We’d like to schedule the alignment call tomorrow at 11:00 PT if that works.

Best,
Sam Patel
Director, Cloud Security
Pinecrest Financial

Attachment: SIG_template_Pinecrest.xlsx

- SOC 2 evidence: we can provide (1) SOC 2 Type II report for the last 12 months (production), (2) for POC we can provide the last Type II + an internal attestation letter covering the POC timeframe. I’ll attach the specific evidence checklist to the MAP as a separate page.
- SIG: thanks for the template — Marissa will start completing the SIG matrix. We typically return a completed SIG within 3–4 business days for standard asks; anything custom will be flagged.

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>

Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Apr 2028 15:18:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: MAP draft & security mileposts (SOC 2 / SIG / DPA / retention)

Hi Sam —

Looping in a short note from legal on the DPA and the mutual indemnity ask. High-level positions:

- DPA scope: Redwood accepts the standard DPA framework with the following clarifications added to the MAP: list of subprocessors, retention windows clearly enumerated (POC: 90 days; Production: configurable 30/90/365), and a defined export/deletion SLA of 72 hours.
- Indemnity: Redwood cannot accept unlimited mutual indemnity tied to third-party acts. We can agree to a mutual, capped indemnity for direct damages arising from a party’s breach of its security obligations; we will include a security remediation plan requirement and insurance representations.
- Acceptance gating: we propose the following minimal acceptance items for production go-live in the MAP: (i) SOC 2 Type II report (last 12 months) delivered to Pinecrest or a signed attestation for POC; (ii) Completed SIG matrix with any open questions documented and an acceptance decision; (iii) Signed DPA or DocuSign envelope executed.

Please let me know if you need redline language now — I can circulate a DPA redline (DPA_redline_v1.docx) by end of day. Also confirming that us-east-1 is available for production and the POC.

Regards,
Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.com

title: Security discovery and vendor questionnaire — MedBridge private deployment options
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Tom Ellis <tellis@medbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Apr 2028 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Security discovery and vendor questionnaire — MedBridge private deployment options

Tom — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side: 

Recap from the call
- Objective: qualify security posture and feasibility for a private/VPC or air-gapped deployment to host models that may process PHI.
- Questions outstanding: SOC 2 status and timeline, GDPR/HIPAA applicability, procurement/BAA timing, and preferred network isolation (VPC peering vs. physically air-gapped).

Immediate asks / next steps
1) I attached a short vendor questionnaire and a network diagram template (see attached). Please have your security or infra team fill the questionnaire and drop a diagram of your expected topology (firewalls, NAT, egress controls, data flow).
2) Tell us whether you consider MedBridge a covered entity for HIPAA or if you'll be a business associate — we need BAA requirements to surface on our side.
3) If air-gap is likely, flag which data classes and expected timelines; we can share an air-gap feasibility checklist.

Timing: if you can return the questionnaire + diagram in 3–5 business days that would be ideal — our security team (Ben and Sergio cc'd) will do a targeted review and come back with any clarifying asks. We'll also scope a short follow-up (30 min) to go over technical items.

Attachments: Vendor questionnaire (Excel), network diagram template (draw.io), short deployment checklist (PDF).

Thanks — I’ll follow up internally to queue security review.

—
Aisha Rahman
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
attach: [MedBridge_Vendor_Questionnaire_v1.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)]

Cc: MedBridge Security <security@medbridgehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Apr 2028 11:33:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Security discovery and vendor questionnaire — MedBridge private deployment options

Hi Aisha — appreciated the clear rundown. Quick answers from our side to help you triage: 

- SOC 2: We are currently SOC 2 Type II in progress; control testing is underway with an expected attestation date of 2028-06-15. We can provide the auditor contact and interim control summaries.
- HIPAA/GDPR: We handle PHI for a subset of our customers and will require a BAA. GDPR applies to our EU users; we have data residency constraints for EU workloads.
- Deployment preference: For workloads that process identifiable PHI we prefer a private deployment inside our VPC with strict egress controls. For some small workloads we may consider an isolated air-gapped option — we need a feasibility checklist.

Security contact: 
- Name: Priya Kapoor (security@medbridgehealth.com) — she’ll take point on the questionnaire and network diagram. I’ve cc’d our security alias above.

I’ll aim to have the filled questionnaire and a basic network sketch back to you by Friday (Apr 13). If that slips, we’ll aim early next week.

Thanks,
Tom

title: [Urgent] p99 backpressure / errors on 32k long-window requests — Borealis Health
preview:
From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@borealishealth.com>
To: aisha_rahman@redwood.ai
Cc: infra-team@borealishealth.com
Date: 2028-04-11T07:10:00Z
Subject: [SEV] Sudden p99 spikes / 500s on long-window (32k) requests

Hi Aisha —

We started seeing elevated p99 latency and intermittent 500 responses from Redwood's hosted API this morning at ~07:02 UTC. The traffic patterns haven't changed on our side; these are the same 32k-context inference calls we've used in production for weeks. Impact: ~12% of inference calls are timing out or returning server errors (affecting live billing runs and downstream ingestion).

Attached: small excerpt of request IDs and a short trace (p99-trace snippet).

Quick details:
- Example request id: req_20280411_BH_0135
- Model pinned: rwd-llama-32k-v2 (hosted)
- Typical p95 before today: ~400ms; p99 usually <1.2s. Now p95 ~1.3s, p99 spiking to 8–12s; some 503/500s.
- No changes to prompt or input size on our side.

This is blocking our nightly pipeline. We need an ETA on mitigation and a short-term workaround. Please escalate — we may need exec attention if this will be unresolved for multiple hours.

Thanks,
Marta Ruiz
Head of Integrations, Borealis Health

Attachment: p99-trace-20280411.txt (excerpt)

Hi Marta — thanks for the alert and for the trace. We're treating this as a priority.

Immediate steps we are taking now:
1) I've looped in our SRE/serving lead (Dev) and product lead (Sean).
2) Dev will run a focused trace across the request IDs you sent and check routing / queue depth for the 32k serving pool.
3) We'll propose a short-term mitigation (config change / fallback) within 30–45 minutes and follow with a rollback plan.

Can you confirm whether you see the failures only for the pinned model (rwd-llama-32k-v2) or also for shorter-window calls? That will help narrow whether this is model-specific or a request-size/queueing issue.

I'll keep you updated every 30 minutes until this is resolved.

— Aisha
Director, Customer Success — Enterprise
Redwood Inference


From: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: 2028-04-11T09:08:00Z
Subject: RE: [SEV] Sudden p99 spikes / 500s on long-window (32k) requests — initial findings

Quick update from the runtime side (initial triage):

What I see:
- Around 07:00 UTC we observed a rapid increase in request concurrency for long-window requests on the hosted pool serving the rwd-llama-32k-v2 variant.
- Our continuous batching pipeline experienced backpressure; batch latency amplified non-linearly once in-memory KV cache hit-rate dropped (evictions due to larger-than-normal KV footprints).
- When KV cache eviction rate increased, we saw additional CPU/GPU context switches while reloading caches, which inflated p99. Some requests hit our hard timeout path and returned 500.

Immediate mitigations applied (07:40 UTC):
- Reduced max concurrent long-window requests per worker for the affected pool to throttle arrival.
- Enabled fallback routing to the 16k-optimized variant for requests that can tolerate truncation (config flag off-by-default).
- Purged a small stale cache segment that was contributing to evictions.

Attachments: metrics-screenshot-07am.png, p99-trace-20280411.txt (full).

Recommended short-term actions for Borealis:
- If acceptable, temporarily set request param allow_truncation=true for non-critical nightly batches to route to 16k variant. This avoids model swap but reduces context length.
- As a platform emergency measure we can roll a hotfix to change the fallback policy for your tenant only (non-global). That takes ~45m with a canary.

Longer term:
- We'll instrument finer-grained KV metrics and deploy an eviction-friendly cache policy for long-window traces; estimate 2–3 sprints for full coverage.

I'll keep watching and will post a follow-up with canary results.

— Dev
Senior SRE, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Thanks for flagging this. We understand the impact and apologize for the disruption. Our engineers have isolated the root cause to KV cache eviction pressure on the 32k serving pool and applied short-term mitigations (throttling and a tenant-only fallback to a 16k-optimized variant).

We will:
- Continue to monitor the canary for the next 2 hours and revert if we detect regressions.
- Provide a timeline for a permanent fix (KV eviction policy + instrumented metrics) within 72 hours.

Aisha on behalf of Redwood Leadership

To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: 2028-04-11T09:08:00Z
Subject: RE: [SEV] Sudden p99 spikes / 500s on long-window (32k) requests — initial findings

Quick update from the runtime side (initial triage):

What I see:
- Around 07:00 UTC we observed a rapid increase in request concurrency for long-window requests on the hosted pool serving the rwd-llama-32k-v2 variant.
- Our continuous batching pipeline experienced backpressure; batch latency amplified non-linearly once in-memory KV cache hit-rate dropped (evictions due to larger-than-normal KV footprints).
- When KV cache eviction rate increased, we saw additional CPU/GPU context switches while reloading caches, which inflated p99. Some requests hit our hard timeout path and returned 500.

Immediate mitigations applied (07:40 UTC):
- Reduced max concurrent long-window requests per worker for the affected pool to throttle arrival.
- Enabled fallback routing to the 16k-optimized variant for requests that can tolerate truncation (config flag off-by-default).
- Purged a small stale cache segment that was contributing to evictions.

Attachments: metrics-screenshot-07am.png, p99-trace-20280411.txt (full).

Recommended short-term actions for Borealis:
- If acceptable, temporarily set request param allow_truncation=true for non-critical nightly batches to route to 16k variant. This avoids model swap but reduces context length.
- As a platform emergency measure we can roll a hotfix to change the fallback policy for your tenant only (non-global). That takes ~45m with a canary.

Longer term:
- We'll instrument finer-grained KV metrics and deploy an eviction-friendly cache policy for long-window traces; estimate 2–3 sprints for full coverage.

I'll keep watching and will post a follow-up with canary results.

— Dev
Senior SRE, Serving Runtime
Redwood Inference

Thanks for flagging this. We understand the impact and apologize for the disruption. Our engineers have isolated the root cause to KV cache eviction pressure on the 32k serving pool and applied short-term mitigations (throttling and a tenant-only fallback to a 16k-optimized variant).

Aisha on behalf of Redwood Leadership

title: Vendor registration / PO matching requirements for Trinova onboarding
preview:
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@trinova.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: accounts-payable@trinova.com
Date: Fri, 6 Apr 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Trinova vendor onboarding: registration + PO matching requirements

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the quick intro on the call earlier. We need Redwood set up as a registered supplier in Trinova's AP system before we can process invoices against customer POs. Attached is our vendor onboarding packet with the required fields. High-level checklist: 

- Complete vendor profile in our portal (legal name, remit address, VAT/EORI or Tax ID, bank routing and account).
- Upload signed tax docs (W-9 for US entity, EORI/VAT certificate for EU entity).
- Provide a PDF sample invoice and a 1-line test invoice showing PO placement.
- Important: Trinova requires the customer PO number to appear in the invoice header and in the e-invoice metadata (EDI field: PO_NUMBER). If PO is missing we will not match and payment will be delayed.
- For EU invoices: if reverse-charge applies, include VAT registration number and set tax line to 0% with reference text 'Reverse charge - Art. 194'.

Portal link and sample packet attached. If you can return the completed packet + test invoice by EOD next week we can complete vendor activation in the next pay cycle.

Attachments (stub): trinova_vendor_onboarding_packet.pdf

Let me know if you prefer to receive the onboarding via DocuSign; we can send an envelope for signatures.

Best,
Elena Morales

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>

Date: 2028-04-05T19:10:00Z
Subject: Re: ACME — MSA redlines + signing logistics

Ben / team —

Thanks for the heads up and the redlines. Quick guidance from Legal:

1) Counter-signing order: Redwood will be the counter-signatory and will sign after Acme completes its signing (i.e., Acme signs first, then Redwood signs). This avoids needing any in-person witness on our side and is our standard for customer MSAs.

2) Signer verification: standard email authentication is sufficient for Acme. If their procurement requests ID verification we can accommodate it, but we should treat that as an exception and coordinate with Acme in advance. No notarization required.

3) Signed artifacts: please upload the final clean Order Form (PDF) to the DocuSign envelope. After completion, Legal needs the executed PDF and the DocuSign certificate-of-completion (including audit trail). Please also push a copy to our S3 archive (see archive path convention) and attach the clean copy to the HubSpot deal record.

4) Signer names/authority: Ben — confirm the exact Acme signers (full legal name + title + email) before we send the envelope. If any signer is signing under POA we need an explicit POA doc attached.

Karthik — recommended routing:
- Lane 1: Acme CFO (signature)
- Lane 2: Acme Legal (acknowledgment)
- Lane 3: Redwood Legal (Marissa) — countersign

Set envelope expiration to 14 days and enable email OTP. After countersign, set system to auto-generate the consolidated executed PDF and deposit to S3. Legal will request the DocuSign audit certificate.

Laura — can you log this in the contract tracker and add the redline version note.

Marissa Cole
Head of Contracts & Legal Ops
Redwood Inference
